657fd7dcde6df0163bbe3c93.leadin: Some have argued that a “neo-” version of this concept could be valid when an organism is considered as a “holobiont,” including the organism as well as all the micro-organisms inside it. For 10 points each:
657fd7dcde6df0163bbe3c93.leadin_sanitized: Some have argued that a "neo-" version of this concept could be valid when an organism is considered as a "holobiont," including the organism as well as all the micro-organisms inside it. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this discredited evolutionary concept, which posits that acquired traits can be inherited. It is exemplified by a giraffe stretching its neck to become taller, and then passing that trait on.
Etienne Serres theorized that embryos re-enact an organism’s stages of evolution, stated as “ontogeny recapitulates” this concept. An evolutionary tree is also called this type of tree.

This evolutionary hypothesis by Alister Hardy describes shellfish-hunting adaptations to explain why humans are hairless bipeds. Elaine Morgan advocated for this idea in The Descent of Woman, which presented it against a contrasting “killer” hypothesis.
parts_sanitized: Name this discredited evolutionary concept, which posits that acquired traits can be inherited. It is exemplified by a giraffe stretching its neck to become taller, and then passing that trait on.
Etienne Serres theorized that embryos re-enact an organism's stages of evolution, stated as "ontogeny recapitulates" this concept. An evolutionary tree is also called this type of tree.

This evolutionary hypothesis by Alister Hardy describes shellfish-hunting adaptations to explain why humans are hairless bipeds. Elaine Morgan advocated for this idea in The Descent of Woman, which presented it against a contrasting "killer" hypothesis.
answers: aquatic ape hypothesis (The “killer ape hypothesis” argues that the evolution of humans was spurred by male war-making.)
answers_sanitized: aquatic ape hypothesis (The "killer ape hypothesis" argues that the evolution of humans was spurred by male war-making.)

A Robert Burton Anatomy of this concept discusses the glass delusion among people’s beliefs that their heads will fall off their shoulders or they have frogs in their stomachs.

657fd7dcde6df0163bbe3c9e.leadin: You are an astronaut in a pop-physics book. For 10 points each, let's talk about the adventures that may befall you.
657fd7dcde6df0163bbe3c9e.leadin_sanitized: You are an astronaut in a pop-physics book. For 10 points each, let's talk about the adventures that may befall you.
parts: You send constant-rate signals to your twin on Earth while traveling at a Lorenz factor of 100, in a formulation of the twin paradox first written up by this physicist, whose namesake dynamics describe molecular systems in solvents.
You blaze past these two nerds, who are, as usual, trying to communicate from opposite ends of the universe using entangled photons - their second-favorite hobby after RSA encryption.

Tragically, you meet this delicious-sounding fate by falling into a black hole. This fate occurs when tidal forces pull more strongly on your feet than your head, stretching you into a long, thin shape.

657fd7ddde6df0163bbe3ca6.question: One plan involving these animals hinged on their ability to eat the water hyacinth and was supported by Robert Broussard. Fritz Duquesne and Ferderick Russell Burnham were assigned to kill each other during the Boer War, but later teamed up on a plan involving these animals. An expensive sterilization program has begun for these animals due to outrage at a photograph by hunters who killed Pepe, one of these animals. Teddy Roosevelt backed a 1910s plan to import these animals to (*) Louisiana to deal with a meat shortage, during which they were advertised as “lake cow bacon.” The descendants of four of these animals at Hacienda Napoles have become an invasive species but contributed to ecotourism. For 10 points, what type of animal is now flourishing in Colombia because they were released from the private zoo of Pablo Escobar?

The descendants of four of these animals at Hacienda Napoles have become an invasive species but contributed to ecotourism. For 10 points, what type of animal is now flourishing in Colombia because they were released from the private zoo of Pablo Escobar?

parts: Name this skill, which is faked by street buskers with a concealed platform and a support hidden in their sleeve.
This saint, the author of The Interior Castle, was supposedly embarrassed by her episodes of levitation and asked other nuns to hold her down.
This “remarkably unclever” man Cupertino was denounced by the Inquisition for levitation, but eventually canonized. Skeptics Robert Smith and Joe Nickell suggested that he was instead a gymnast.

657fd7ddde6df0163bbe3cd0.question: A long-nosed woman and a statue with 6 breasts appear in this artist’s Visit to the Plastic Surgeon, and men walk on their beards in this artist’s Hairy Locomotion. In one painting by this artist, the title figure with a five-pointed star for a head is observed by 20 identically grey-clad onlookers. In another painting by this artist, a woman draped in cloth that blends into a black-and-white tiled floor turns a pulley. This artist of The Juggler and Alchemy or the Useless Science created a work in which women in a tower weave cloth that flows out to become the landscape beyond - (*) Oedipa Maas weeps in front of that painting, Embroidering the Earth’s Mantle, in The Crying of Lot 49. Kati Horna, Leonora Carrington, and this artist were known as “the three witches” while living in Mexico City. For 10 points, name this Spanish surrealist.

This discipline, founded by Abbe Jean Michon, uses the (*) t-bar and separates elements into the intellectual “upper zone,” the personal “middle zone,” and the practical “lower zone,” based on “copybook” divisions. This novelist, who won for The Age of Magic, wrote Starbook and The Freedom Artist. This author’s most well-known novel involves an abiku spirit clinging to earth to observe his boxer father.

This man won in 2007 for The Castle in the Forest. Dorothy Parker called this author “the man who can’t spell ‘fuck’” after he was told to replace it with the euphemism “fug” in his novel The Naked and the Dead.
